symbols: God in blue robes — the Creator giving life and commandments
nude Adam and Eve — innocence before and shame after the Fall
serpent or forbidden tree — the source of temptation
angels — divine witnesses and eventual agents of expulsion
landscape with animals — the goodness of creation before the curse
multiple scenes in one panel — the unfolding narrative of paradise, Fall, and consequences
description: A wide horizontal panel depicting multiple scenes of the Creation and Fall narrative in a continuous landscape. At center, a robed figure (God) stands with arms raised, surrounded by angels in white. Nude figures (Adam and Eve) appear at various points: at left, Adam appears to be newly created on the ground; at center, the couple stands before God; at right, they are near a tree in what may be the temptation scene. Animals populate the landscape at left. The setting is a lush green landscape with hills, water, and trees under a blue sky. The panel is shown in its gilded frame.
theological_description: Albertinelli presents the entire Genesis narrative of creation and Fall in a single panoramic composition, a common format in Renaissance predella panels. The sequence moves from left to right: the creation of Adam from the dust (Gen 2:7), God presenting Eve to Adam (Gen 2:22), God giving the commandment about the forbidden tree (Gen 2:16-17), the temptation and Fall (Gen 3:6), and the consequences. God's central presence with raised arms embodies the creative Word: 'Let us make man in our image' (Gen 1:26). The angels attending reflect the plural 'us' that early theologians interpreted as the Trinity. The lush landscape represents the 'very good' creation (Gen 1:31) before sin corrupted it. The narrative arc from creation through Fall captures the fundamental biblical drama: humanity made for communion with God, choosing autonomy, and facing the consequences — yet within a creation that remains beautiful, pointing toward redemption.

scripture_refs.reason: God formed man from the dust — the creation of Adam shown at left
God made a woman and brought her to the man — Eve presented to Adam
She took of the fruit and ate — the temptation scene at right
Let us make man in our image — God with angels at center
Sin came into the world through one man — the Fall's universal consequences
